sections.paragraph:
fut. יַחְמֹל inf. חֻמְלָה Eze 16:5, to be mild, gentle. (Arab. with the letters transposed حَلُمَ to be gentle, longsuffering, حِلْمُ μακροθυμία, حَلِيمُ gentle. The primary idea is that of softness, and this signification is preserved in the Greek, ἁμαλός, ἀμαλός, ἁπαλός.) Hence-

(1) to pity, to have compassion on, followed by עַל of pers. Exo 2:6 1Sa 23:21.

(2) to spare, followed by עַל 1Sa 15:3, 15 1Sa 15:15 2Sa 21:7 2Ch 36:15, 17 2Ch 36:17followed by אֶל Isa 9:18 also, to be sparing of any thing, to use sparingly, followed by אֶל Jer 50:14 לְ with inf. 2Sa 12:4 עַל Job 20:13 Eze 36:21, “I will be sparing of my holy name,” I will care for its honour.

Hence מַחְמָל (unless it be from the Arab. حمل) [also pr.n. חָמוּל] and-
